Map of Hokkaido
| Latitude_deg = 42 | Latitude_min = 45 | Latitude_sec = 23.3 | Hemisphere (N/S) = N | Longitude_deg = 141 | Longitude_min = 47 | Longitude_sec = 50.6 | Hemisphere (E/W) = E | Access = From {{ill|Hokkaido Railway Company|ja|北海道旅客鉄道|lt=JR Hokkaido|lt_ja=JR北海道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|12=draft|12=draft}} by car approx. 5 minutes
From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|12=draft|12=draft}} by car approx. 15 minutes | Spring_type = Sulfur-containing - Sodium - Chloride/Bicarbonate spring | Spring_temperature = 14.8°C | Discharge_volume = | pH = 8.3 | Fluid_classification = Weakly alkaline | Osmotic_classification = Hypotonic | Number_of_accommodations = 1 | Total_capacity = | Annual_visitors = | Statistics_year = | External_links = [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official site introduction page]
[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Hayarai Kitamachi, {{ill|Abira Town|ja|安平町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yufutsu District|ja|勇払郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|12=draft|12=draft}} - Sodium - {{ill|chloride spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|lt=Chloride|lt_ja=塩化物|12=draft|12=draft}}/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|temperature|ja|温度|12=draft|12=draft}} 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a {{ill|hydrogen sulfide|ja|硫化水素|12=draft|12=draft}} odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|12=draft|12=draft}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|12=draft|12=draft}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|12=draft|12=draft}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|12=draft|12=draft}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|12=draft|12=draft}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|12=draft|12=draft}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Abira Town]] [[Category:Facilities opened in 1902]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]]

The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ ふる里の名湯 鶴の湯温泉] |特記事項 = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|浴室]] '''鶴の湯温泉'''（つるのゆおんせん）は、[[北海道]][[勇払郡]][[安平町]]早来北町にある[[温泉]]。 == 泉質 == * [[硫黄泉|含硫黄]] - ナトリウム - [[塩化物泉|塩化物]]・炭酸水素塩泉（低張性弱アルカリ性冷鉱泉） ** [[源泉]][[温度]] 14.8度、pH 8.3（弱アルカリ性） ** 淡黄色透明、無味、[[硫化水素]]臭 == 温泉地 == [[宿泊施設]]と[[日帰り入浴施設]]を兼ねた施設が1軒がある。 温泉施設は内風呂のみで。加温濾過循環・[[掛け流し]]併用。 == 歴史 == 明治の初めに、鶴が水浴びをして傷を癒していたことから発見された。[[1902年]]（[[明治]]35年）開湯。安平町及び鶴の湯温泉の主張によれば、北海道では[[湯の川温泉 (北海道)|湯の川温泉]]に次いで2番目に長い歴史を持つとされる。しかし北海道にはそれ以前に開湯した温泉が複数あり、事実と反する。 旧[[早来町]]により温泉の再開発が計画されたが、町財政の悪化から[[2006年]]（[[平成]]18年）度以降へ先送りとされ、再開発計画が実質頓挫。早来町と[[追分町]]の合併問題の足枷の一部となっていた。 [[2008年]]（平成20年）12月環境総合大手の三友グループ(本社神奈川)に売却、温泉営業は継承された。施設を新築した後、[[2010年]]（平成22年）[[8月10日]]に営業再開した。 == アクセス == * 鉄道：[[室蘭本線]][[早来駅]]より車で約5分 * 飛行機：[[新千歳空港]]から車で約15分 * バス：[[あつまバス]]「北町」停留所下車徒歩約5分 == 関連項目 == * [[日本の温泉地一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru 安平町公式サイト内の紹介ページ] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ ふる里の名湯 鶴の湯温泉] {{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つるのゆおんせん}} {{onsen-stub}} [[Category:北海道の温泉]] [[Category:安平町の建築物]] [[Category:1902年開業の施設]]

{{infobox Onsen | Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen (鶴の湯温泉) | image = Tsurunoyu spa 2011.JPG | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Yufutsu District|ja|勇払郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Abira Town|ja|安平町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hayarai Kitamachi 5 {{Location map many | Hokkaido South#Japan Hokkaido | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 42.756472 | lon_deg = 141.797389 | pos = right | label = Tsurunoyu Onsen (鶴の湯温泉) }}

| Latitude_deg = 42 | Latitude_min = 45 | Latitude_sec = 23.3 | Hemisphere (N/S) = N | Longitude_deg = 141 | Longitude_min = 47 | Longitude_sec = 50.6 | Hemisphere (E/W) = E | Access = From {{ill|Hokkaido Railway Company|ja|北海道旅客鉄道|lt=JR Hokkaido|lt_ja=JR北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by car approx. 5 minutes
From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by car approx. 15 minutes | Spring_type = Sulfur-containing - Sodium - Chloride/Bicarbonate spring | Spring_temperature = 14.8°C | Discharge_volume = | pH = 8.3 | Fluid_classification = Weakly alkaline | Osmotic_classification = Hypotonic | Number_of_accommodations = 1 | Total_capacity = | Annual_visitors = | Statistics_year = | External_links = [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official site introduction page]
[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Hayarai Kitamachi, {{ill|Abira Town|ja|安平町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yufutsu District|ja|勇払郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Sodium - {{ill|chloride spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|lt=Chloride|lt_ja=塩化物|qq=draft|qq=draft}}/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|temperature|ja|温度|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a {{ill|hydrogen sulfide|ja|硫化水素|qq=draft|qq=draft}} odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Abira Town]] [[Category:Facilities opened in 1902]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Abira Town]] [[Category:Facilities opened in 1902]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]]

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Hayarai Kitamachi, {{ill|Abira Town|ja|安平町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yufutsu District|ja|勇払郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Sodium - {{ill|chloride spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|lt=Chloride|lt_ja=塩化物|qq=draft|qq=draft}}/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|temperature|ja|温度|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a {{ill|hydrogen sulfide|ja|硫化水素|qq=draft|qq=draft}} odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Abira Town]] [[Category:Facilities opened in 1902]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]]

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Hayarai Kitamachi, {{ill|Abira Town|ja|安平町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yufutsu District|ja|勇払郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Sodium - {{ill|chloride spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|lt=Chloride|lt_ja=塩化物|qq=draft|qq=draft}}/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|temperature|ja|温度|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a {{ill|hydrogen sulfide|ja|硫化水素|qq=draft|qq=draft}} odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Abira Town]] [[Category:Facilities opened in 1902]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]]

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Hayarai Kitamachi, {{ill|Abira Town|ja|安平町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yufutsu District|ja|勇払郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Sodium - {{ill|chloride spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|lt=Chloride|lt_ja=塩化物|qq=draft|qq=draft}}/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|temperature|ja|温度|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a {{ill|hydrogen sulfide|ja|硫化水素|qq=draft|qq=draft}} odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Abira Town]] [[Category:Facilities opened in 1902]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]]

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|qq=}} located in Hayarai Kitamachi, {{ill|Abira Town|ja|安平町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abira Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Yufutsu District|ja|勇払郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufutsu District|qq=}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - {{ill|chloride spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|lt=Chloride|lt_ja=塩化物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chloride spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|qq=}}/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} {{ill|temperature|ja|温度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temperature|qq=}} 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a {{ill|hydrogen sulfide|ja|硫化水素|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hydrogen sulfide|qq=}} odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1902|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|qq=}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayarai Town|qq=}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiwake Town|qq=}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 10|qq=}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroran Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaragi Station|qq=}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Chitose Airport|qq=}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/List of hot springs in Japan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Abira Town]] [[Category:Facilities opened in 1902]] [[Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1902|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|qq=}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayarai Town|qq=}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiwake Town|qq=}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 10|qq=}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroran Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaragi Station|qq=}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Chitose Airport|qq=}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/List of hot springs in Japan|qq=}} == External Links == * From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Chitose Airport|qq=}} by car approx. 15 minutes | Spring_type = Sulfur-containing - Sodium - Chloride/Bicarbonate spring | Spring_temperature = 14.8°C | Discharge_volume = | pH = 8.3 | Fluid_classification = Weakly alkaline | Osmotic_classification = Hypotonic | Number_of_accommodations = 1 | Total_capacity = | Annual_visitors = | Statistics_year = | External_links = [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official site introduction page]
[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|qq=}} located in Hayarai Kitamachi, {{ill|Abira Town|ja|安平町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abira Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Yufutsu District|ja|勇払郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufutsu District|qq=}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - {{ill|chloride spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|lt=Chloride|lt_ja=塩化物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chloride spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|qq=}}/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} {{ill|temperature|ja|温度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temperature|qq=}} 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a {{ill|hydrogen sulfide|ja|硫化水素|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hydrogen sulfide|qq=}} odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1902|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|qq=}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayarai Town|qq=}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiwake Town|qq=}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 10|qq=}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{infobox Onsen | Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen (鶴の湯温泉) | image = Tsurunoyu spa 2011.JPG | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}}{{ill|Yufutsu District|ja|勇払郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufutsu District|qq=}}{{ill|Abira Town|ja|安平町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abira Town|qq=}} Hayarai Kitamachi 5 {{Location map many | Hokkaido South#Japan Hokkaido | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 42.756472 | lon_deg = 141.797389 | pos = right | label = Tsurunoyu Onsen (鶴の湯温泉) }}
Map of Hokkaido
| Latitude_deg = 42 | Latitude_min = 45 | Latitude_sec = 23.3 | Hemisphere (N/S) = N | Longitude_deg = 141 | Longitude_min = 47 | Longitude_sec = 50.6 | Hemisphere (E/W) = E | Access = From {{ill|Hokkaido Railway Company|ja|北海道旅客鉄道|lt=JR Hokkaido|lt_ja=JR北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Railway Company|qq=}} {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroran Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaragi Station|qq=}} by car approx. 5 minutes
From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Chitose Airport|qq=}} by car approx. 15 minutes | Spring_type = Sulfur-containing - Sodium - Chloride/Bicarbonate spring | Spring_temperature = 14.8°C | Discharge_volume = | pH = 8.3 | Fluid_classification = Weakly alkaline | Osmotic_classification = Hypotonic | Number_of_accommodations = 1 | Total_capacity = | Annual_visitors = | Statistics_year = | External_links = [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official site introduction page]
[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|qq=}} located in Hayarai Kitamachi, {{ill|Abira Town|ja|安平町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abira Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Yufutsu District|ja|勇払郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufutsu District|qq=}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - {{ill|chloride spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|lt=Chloride|lt_ja=塩化物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chloride spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|qq=}}/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} {{ill|temperature|ja|温度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temperature|qq=}} 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a {{ill|hydrogen sulfide|ja|硫化水素|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hydrogen sulfide|qq=}} odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1902|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|qq=}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayarai Town|qq=}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiwake Town|qq=}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 10|qq=}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroran Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaragi Station|qq=}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Chitose Airport|qq=}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/List of hot springs in Japan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{citation 1902]] [[Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1902|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|qq=}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayarai Town|qq=}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiwake Town|qq=}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 10|qq=}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroran Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaragi Station|qq=}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Chitose Airport|qq=}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/List of hot springs in Japan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Abira Town]] [[Category:Facilities opened in 1902]] [[Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|qq=}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayarai Town|qq=}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiwake Town|qq=}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 10|qq=}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroran Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaragi Station|qq=}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Chitose Airport|qq=}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/List of hot springs in Japan|qq=}} == External Links == * needed|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Abira Town]] [[Category:Facilities opened in | Latitude_deg = 42 | Latitude_min = 45 | Latitude_sec = 23.3 | Hemisphere (N/S) = N | Longitude_deg = 141 | Longitude_min = 47 | Longitude_sec = 50.6 | Hemisphere (E/W) = E | Access = From {{ill|Hokkaido Railway Company|ja|北海道旅客鉄道|lt=JR Hokkaido|lt_ja=JR北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Railway Company|qq=}} {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroran Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaragi Station|qq=}} by car approx. 5 minutes
From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Chitose Airport|qq=}} by car approx. 15 minutes | Spring_type = Sulfur-containing - Sodium - Chloride/Bicarbonate spring | Spring_temperature = 14.8°C | Discharge_volume = | pH = 8.3 | Fluid_classification = Weakly alkaline | Osmotic_classification = Hypotonic | Number_of_accommodations = 1 | Total_capacity = | Annual_visitors = | Statistics_year = | External_links = [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official site introduction page]
[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|qq=}} located in Hayarai Kitamachi, {{ill|Abira Town|ja|安平町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abira Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Yufutsu District|ja|勇払郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufutsu District|qq=}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - {{ill|chloride spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|lt=Chloride|lt_ja=塩化物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chloride spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|qq=}}/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} {{ill|temperature|ja|温度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temperature|qq=}} 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a {{ill|hydrogen sulfide|ja|硫化水素|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hydrogen sulfide|qq=}} odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1902|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|qq=}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayarai Town|qq=}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiwake Town|qq=}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 10|qq=}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroran Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaragi From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Chitose Airport|qq=}} by car approx. 15 minutes | Spring_type = Sulfur-containing - Sodium - Chloride/Bicarbonate spring | Spring_temperature = 14.8°C | Discharge_volume = | pH = 8.3 | Fluid_classification = Weakly alkaline | Osmotic_classification = Hypotonic | Number_of_accommodations = 1 | Total_capacity = | Annual_visitors = | Statistics_year = | External_links = [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official site introduction page]
[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|qq=}} located in Hayarai Kitamachi, {{ill|Abira Town|ja|安平町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abira Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Yufutsu District|ja|勇払郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufutsu District|qq=}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - {{ill|chloride spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|lt=Chloride|lt_ja=塩化物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chloride spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|qq=}}/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} {{ill|temperature|ja|温度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temperature|qq=}} 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a {{ill|hydrogen sulfide|ja|硫化水素|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hydrogen sulfide|qq=}} odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1902|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|qq=}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayarai Town|qq=}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiwake Town|qq=}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 10|qq=}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroran Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaragi Station|qq=}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Chitose Airport|qq=}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/List of hot springs in Japan|qq=}} == External Links == * The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1902|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|qq=}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayarai Town|qq=}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiwake Town|qq=}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 10|qq=}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|ja|室蘭本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroran Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Hayaragi Station|ja|早来駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaragi Station|qq=}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|ja|新千歳空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Chitose Airport|qq=}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|ja|日本の温泉地一覧|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|1=List of hot springs in Japan|2=ja|3=日本の温泉地一覧|4=zh|5=日本溫泉列表|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/List of hot springs in Japan|qq=}} == External Links == * In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 10|qq=}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Hot [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|qq=}} located in Hayarai Kitamachi, {{ill|Abira Town|ja|安平町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abira Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Yufutsu District|ja|勇払郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufutsu District|qq=}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|ja|北海道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido|qq=}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - {{ill|chloride spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|lt=Chloride|lt_ja=塩化物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chloride spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|qq=}}/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} {{ill|temperature|ja|温度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temperature|qq=}} 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a {{ill|hydrogen sulfide|ja|硫化水素|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hydrogen sulfide|qq=}} odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|ja|1902年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1902|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|湯の川温泉 (北海道)|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|lt_ja=湯の川温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|qq=}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayarai Town|ja|早来町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayarai Town|qq=}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|ja|2006年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake Town|ja|追分町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiwake Town|qq=}}. In December {{ill|2008|ja|2008年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|ja|2010年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 10|qq=}}. == Access == * Railway: From
