[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Abira Town]] [[Category:Facilities opened in 1902]] [[Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)-5]]

After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Abira Town]] [[Category:Facilities opened in 1902]] [[Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)-5]] [[Category:1902年開業の施設]]

From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]] by car approx. 15 minutes | Spring_type = Sulfur-containing - Sodium - Chloride/Bicarbonate spring | Spring_temperature = 14.8°C | Discharge_volume = | pH = 8.3 | Fluid_classification = Weakly alkaline | Osmotic_classification = Hypotonic | Number_of_accommodations = 1 | Total_capacity = | Annual_visitors = | Statistics_year = | External_links = [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official site introduction page]
[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|1=Day-trip bathing facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Abira Town]] [[Category:Facilities opened in 1902]] [[Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)-5]] [[Category:1902年開業の施設]] [[Category:Hot springs of Hokkaido]]

It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot Springs of Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings in Abira Town]] [[Category:Facilities opened in 1902]] [[Category:Tsurunoyu Onsen]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)-5]] [[Category:1902年開業の施設]] [[Category:Hot springs of Hokkaido]] [[Category:安平町の建築物]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]]

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)-5]] [[Category:1902年開業の施設]] [[Category:安平町の建築物]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]]

After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)-5]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen (鶴の湯温泉) | image = Tsurunoyu spa 2011.JPG | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]][[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]][[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]] Hayarai Kitamachi 5 {{Location map many | Hokkaido South#Japan Hokkaido | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 42.756472 | lon_deg = 141.797389 | pos = right | label = Tsurunoyu Onsen (鶴の湯温泉) }}

| Latitude_deg = 42 | Latitude_min = 45 | Latitude_sec = 23.3 | Hemisphere (N/S) = N | Longitude_deg = 141 | Longitude_min = 47 | Longitude_sec = 50.6 | Hemisphere (E/W) = E | Access = From [[:en:Hokkaido Railway Company|JR Hokkaido]] [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]] by car approx. 5 minutes
From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]] by car approx. 15 minutes | Spring_type = Sulfur-containing - Sodium - Chloride/Bicarbonate spring | Spring_temperature = 14.8°C | Discharge_volume = | pH = 8.3 | Fluid_classification = Weakly alkaline | Osmotic_classification = Hypotonic | Number_of_accommodations = 1 | Total_capacity = | Annual_visitors = | Statistics_year = | External_links = [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official site introduction page]
[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[ja:%E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)-5]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[sn:E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)-5]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[sn:E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)-5]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]][[Category:qq]]

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48835818}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[sn:E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)-5]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]][[Category:qq]]

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48835818}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Kakenagashi|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakenagashi|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[sn:E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)-5]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]

After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[sn:E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)-5]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] {{wikidata link|Q11255725}}

From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]] by car approx. 15 minutes | Spring_type = Sulfur-containing - Sodium - Chloride/Bicarbonate spring | Spring_temperature = 14.8°C | Discharge_volume = | pH = 8.3 | Fluid_classification = Weakly alkaline | Osmotic_classification = Hypotonic | Number_of_accommodations = 1 | Total_capacity = | Annual_visitors = | Statistics_year = | External_links = [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official site introduction page]
[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48835818}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]]}} [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)]] [[sn:E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89_(%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[sn:E9%B6%B4%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89 (%E5%8C%97%E6%B5%B7%E9%81%93)]] [[sn:Tsuruno-yu Onsen (Hokkaido)-5]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11255725}}

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|qq=|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48835818}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|qq=|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11255725}}

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48835818}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}}

[[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11255725]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:31, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Tsurunoyu Onsen (鶴の湯温泉) | image = Tsurunoyu spa 2011.JPG | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]][[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]][[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]] Hayarai Kitamachi 5 {{Location map many | Hokkaido South#Japan Hokkaido | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 42.756472 | lon_deg = 141.797389 | pos = right | label = Tsurunoyu Onsen (鶴の湯温泉) }}
Map of Hokkaido
| Latitude_deg = 42 | Latitude_min = 45 | Latitude_sec = 23.3 | Hemisphere (N/S) = N | Longitude_deg = 141 | Longitude_min = 47 | Longitude_sec = 50.6 | Hemisphere (E/W) = E | Access = From [[:en:Hokkaido Railway Company|JR Hokkaido]] [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]] by car approx. 5 minutes
From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]] by car approx. 15 minutes | Spring_type = Sulfur-containing - Sodium - Chloride/Bicarbonate spring | Spring_temperature = 14.8°C | Discharge_volume = | pH = 8.3 | Fluid_classification = Weakly alkaline | Osmotic_classification = Hypotonic | Number_of_accommodations = 1 | Total_capacity = | Annual_visitors = | Statistics_year = | External_links = [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official site introduction page]
[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48835818}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsurunoyu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48835818}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsurunoyu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsurunoyu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsuruno-yu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48835818}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsuruno-yu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}}

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsuruno-yu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48835818}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsuruno-yu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}}

It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}} [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsuruno-yu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48835818}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsuruno-yu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}} [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]
