According to claims by Abira Town and Tsuruno-yu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}} [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsuruno-yu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in Hayarai Kitamachi, [[:en:Abira, Hokkaido|Abira Town]], [[:en:Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|Yufutsu District]], [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]]. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride]]/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} [[:en:Temperature|temperature]] 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a [[:en:Hydrogen sulfide|hydrogen sulfide]] odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48835818}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in [[:en:1902|1902]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsuruno-yu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after [[:en:Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|Yunokawa Onsen]]. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former [[:en:Hayakita, Hokkaido|Hayarai Town]], but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year [[:en:2006|2006]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and [[:en:Oiwake, Hokkaido|Oiwake Town]]. In December [[:en:2008|2008]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on [[:en:2010|2010]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]] 22) on [[:en:August 10|August 10]]. == Access == * Railway: From [[:en:Muroran Main Line|Muroran Main Line]] [[:en:Hayakita Station|Hayaragi Station]], about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From [[:en:New Chitose Airport|New Chitose Airport]], about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of hot springs in Japan|List of hot springs in Japan]] == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}} [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|en|1902|WD=Q2043|lt=1902}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsuruno-yu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|en|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|WD=Q3916336|lt=Yunokawa Onsen}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayakita, Hokkaido|en|Hayakita, Hokkaido|WD=Q782607|lt=Hayarai Town}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei era}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake, Hokkaido|en|Oiwake, Hokkaido|WD=Q774645|lt=Oiwake Town}}. In December {{ill|2008|en|2008|WD=Q2004|lt=2008}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei era}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei era}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|en|August 10|WD=Q2779|lt=August 10}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|en|Muroran Main Line|WD=Q1074252|lt=Muroran Main Line}} {{ill|Hayakita Station|en|Hayakita Station|WD=Q5685978|lt=Hayaragi Station}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|en|New Chitose Airport|WD=Q908296|lt=New Chitose Airport}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of hot springs in Japan|en|List of hot springs in Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of hot springs in Japan}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}} [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

[[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] {{infobox Onsen | Name = Tsuruno-yu Onsen (鶴の湯温泉) | image = Tsurunoyu spa 2011.JPG | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}{{ill|Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|en|Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1185954|lt=Yufutsu District}}{{ill|Abira, Hokkaido|en|Abira, Hokkaido|WD=Q1354084|lt=Abira Town}} Hayarai Kitamachi 5 {{Location map many | Hokkaido South#Japan Hokkaido | relief = 1 | caption = | width = 220 | float = center | lat_deg = 42.756472 | lon_deg = 141.797389 | pos = right | label = Tsuruno-yu Onsen (鶴の湯温泉) }}
Map of Hokkaido
| Latitude_deg = 42 | Latitude_min = 45 | Latitude_sec = 23.3 | Hemisphere (N/S) = N | Longitude_deg = 141 | Longitude_min = 47 | Longitude_sec = 50.6 | Hemisphere (E/W) = E | Access = From {{ill|Hokkaido Railway Company|en|Hokkaido Railway Company|WD=Q498930|lt=JR Hokkaido}} {{ill|Muroran Main Line|en|Muroran Main Line|WD=Q1074252|lt=Muroran Main Line}} {{ill|Hayakita Station|en|Hayakita Station|WD=Q5685978|lt=Hayaragi Station}} by car approx. 5 minutes
From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|en|New Chitose Airport|WD=Q908296|lt=New Chitose Airport}} by car approx. 15 minutes | Spring_type = Sulfur-containing - Sodium - Chloride/Bicarbonate spring | Spring_temperature = 14.8°C | Discharge_volume = | pH = 8.3 | Fluid_classification = Weakly alkaline | Osmotic_classification = Hypotonic | Number_of_accommodations = 1 | Total_capacity = | Annual_visitors = | Statistics_year = | External_links = [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official site introduction page]
[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsuruno-yu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in Hayarai Kitamachi, {{ill|Abira, Hokkaido|en|Abira, Hokkaido|WD=Q1354084|lt=Abira Town}}, {{ill|Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|en|Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1185954|lt=Yufutsu District}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Chloride}}/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} {{ill|Temperature|en|Temperature|WD=Q11466|lt=temperature}} 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a {{ill|Hydrogen sulfide|en|Hydrogen sulfide|WD=Q170591|lt=hydrogen sulfide}} odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48835818}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|en|1902|WD=Q2043|lt=1902}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsuruno-yu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|en|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|WD=Q3916336|lt=Yunokawa Onsen}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayakita, Hokkaido|en|Hayakita, Hokkaido|WD=Q782607|lt=Hayarai Town}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei era}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake, Hokkaido|en|Oiwake, Hokkaido|WD=Q774645|lt=Oiwake Town}}. In December {{ill|2008|en|2008|WD=Q2004|lt=2008}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei era}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei era}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|en|August 10|WD=Q2779|lt=August 10}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|en|Muroran Main Line|WD=Q1074252|lt=Muroran Main Line}} {{ill|Hayakita Station|en|Hayakita Station|WD=Q5685978|lt=Hayaragi Station}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|en|New Chitose Airport|WD=Q908296|lt=New Chitose Airport}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of hot springs in Japan|en|List of hot springs in Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of hot springs in Japan}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}} [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:pages]]

| Latitude_deg = 42 | Latitude_min = 45 | Latitude_sec = 23.3 | Hemisphere (N/S) = N | Longitude_deg = 141 | Longitude_min = 47 | Longitude_sec = 50.6 | Hemisphere (E/W) = E | Access = From {{ill|Hokkaido Railway Company|en|Hokkaido Railway Company|WD=Q498930|lt=JR Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido Railway Company}} {{ill|Muroran Main Line|en|Muroran Main Line|WD=Q1074252|lt=Muroran Main Line|1=Muroran Main Line}} {{ill|Hayakita Station|en|Hayakita Station|WD=Q5685978|lt=Hayaragi Station|1=Hayakita Station}} by car approx. 5 minutes
From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|en|New Chitose Airport|WD=Q908296|lt=New Chitose Airport|1=New Chitose Airport}} by car approx. 15 minutes | Spring_type = Sulfur-containing - Sodium - Chloride/Bicarbonate spring | Spring_temperature = 14.8°C | Discharge_volume = | pH = 8.3 | Fluid_classification = Weakly alkaline | Osmotic_classification = Hypotonic | Number_of_accommodations = 1 | Total_capacity = | Annual_visitors = | Statistics_year = | External_links = [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official site introduction page]
[http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] | Special_notes = }} [[File:Tsurunoyu spa uchiyu.JPG|thumb|250px|Bathing Room]] '''Tsuruno-yu Onsen''' (鶴の湯温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in Hayarai Kitamachi, {{ill|Abira, Hokkaido|en|Abira, Hokkaido|WD=Q1354084|lt=Abira Town|1=Abira, Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|en|Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1185954|lt=Yufutsu District|1=Yūfutsu District, Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}. == Water Quality == * {{ill|sulfur spring|ja|硫黄泉|lt=Sulfur-containing|lt_ja=含硫黄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur spring|qq=}} - Sodium - {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Chloride|1=Brine spring}}/Bicarbonate spring (hypotonic, weakly alkaline, cold mineral spring) ** {{ill|spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} {{ill|Temperature|en|Temperature|WD=Q11466|lt=temperature|1=Temperature}} 14.8°C, pH 8.3 (weakly alkaline) ** Light yellow, transparent, tasteless, with a {{ill|Hydrogen sulfide|en|Hydrogen sulfide|WD=Q170591|lt=hydrogen sulfide|1=Hydrogen sulfide}} odor == Hot Spring Resort == There is one facility that serves as both a {{ill|accommodation facility|ja|宿泊施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accommodation facility|1=accommodation facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48835818}} and a {{ill|day-use bath facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/day-use bath facility|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}. The hot spring facility features only an indoor bath, utilizing both a reheated, filtered circulation system and the {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} method. == History == It was discovered in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} when a crane was observed bathing to heal its wounds. It opened in {{ill|1902|en|1902|WD=Q2043|lt=1902|1=1902}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 35). According to claims by Abira Town and Tsuruno-yu Onsen, it is said to have the second longest history in Hokkaido after {{ill|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|en|Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)|WD=Q3916336|lt=Yunokawa Onsen|1=Yunokawa Onsen (Hokkaido)}}. However, several hot springs in Hokkaido were opened before this, contradicting the claim. A redevelopment of the hot spring was planned by the former {{ill|Hayakita, Hokkaido|en|Hayakita, Hokkaido|WD=Q782607|lt=Hayarai Town|1=Hayakita, Hokkaido}}, but due to worsening municipal finances, it was postponed to after the fiscal year {{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei era|1=Heisei era}} 18) and the redevelopment plan essentially collapsed. It also became one of the stumbling blocks in the merger issues between Hayarai Town and {{ill|Oiwake, Hokkaido|en|Oiwake, Hokkaido|WD=Q774645|lt=Oiwake Town|1=Oiwake, Hokkaido}}. In December {{ill|2008|en|2008|WD=Q2004|lt=2008|1=2008}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei era|1=Heisei era}} 20), it was sold to the major environmental conglomerate Sanyu Group (headquartered in Kanagawa), and the hot spring operations were inherited. After constructing a new facility, it reopened on {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010|1=2010}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei era|1=Heisei era}} 22) on {{ill|August 10|en|August 10|WD=Q2779|lt=August 10|1=August 10}}. == Access == * Railway: From {{ill|Muroran Main Line|en|Muroran Main Line|WD=Q1074252|lt=Muroran Main Line|1=Muroran Main Line}} {{ill|Hayakita Station|en|Hayakita Station|WD=Q5685978|lt=Hayaragi Station|1=Hayakita Station}}, about 5 minutes by car * Airplane: From {{ill|New Chitose Airport|en|New Chitose Airport|WD=Q908296|lt=New Chitose Airport|1=New Chitose Airport}}, about 15 minutes by car * Bus: From {{ill|Atsuma Bus|ja|あつまバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuma Bus|1=Atsuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257736}}, get off at the "Kitamachi" stop and walk approximately 5 minutes == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of hot springs in Japan|en|List of hot springs in Japan|WD=Q11086467|lt=List of hot springs in Japan|1=List of hot springs in Japan}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.abira.lg.jp/tour_03.php#tsuru Abira Town official website introduction page] * [http://www.tsurunoyu.jp/ Famous hometown hot spring, Tsuruno-yu Onsen] {{onsen-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)|version=88868212|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鶴の湯温泉 (北海道)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675862}} [[Category:Architecture of Abira Town]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1902]] [[Category:Hot springs of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]
